message:
fix(context): git worktree support for repo revision, packed-refs fallback, cache key completeness

internal/cli/context.go:
- Add resolveGitDir() to handle git worktree checkouts where .git is a
  file containing "gitdir: <path>" instead of a directory, enabling
  correct HEAD resolution in worktree-based agent sessions
- Add readGitRevision() with packed-refs fallback for repos that have
  been gc'd — reads refs/heads/<branch> first, falls back to parsing
  packed-refs file when loose ref is missing
- Refactor getRepoRev() to use the new helpers, fixing silent "unknown"
  returns in worktree environments that caused unnecessary cache misses

internal/cli/context_inject_test.go:
- Add TestGetRepoRev_DirectoryGitDir: validates standard .git directory
- Add TestGetRepoRev_WorktreeGitDirFile: validates worktree .git file
  with relative gitdir path resolution
- Add TestGetRepoRev_PackedRefsFallback: validates packed-refs parsing
  when loose ref file is absent

internal/context/pack.go:
- Include all BuildOptions fields in cacheKey hash: Task, CorrelationID,
  ProjectDir, SessionID, Files, plus BudgetAllocation — previously only
  RepoRev/BeadID/AgentType/IncludeMSSkills were hashed, causing cache
  collisions between packs with different tasks or allocation budgets
- Use null-byte delimited writes to prevent hash collisions from field
  value concatenation (e.g., "ab"+"c" vs "a"+"bc")

internal/context/pack_test.go:
- Update all existing cacheKey() calls to pass allocation parameter
- Add tests for Task, Files, ProjectDir, SessionID, CorrelationID, and
  allocation sensitivity in cache key generation
- Add TestBuildDoesNotReuseCacheAcrossDistinctInputs: validates that
  Build() returns fresh packs when task/correlation change
- Add TestBuildDoesNotReuseCacheAcrossAllocationChanges: validates that
  different BudgetAllocation settings produce distinct cache entries

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
